title: Floral Tender Portuguese Peas Dumplings
description: Light, fragrant dumplings filled with sweet peas and a hint of rose water, served soaked in a savory vegetable stock – a gentle hangover cure that's egg‑free and perfectly Portuguese.

ingredients.name: dried green peas
ingredients.notes: soak overnight or use fresh peas, thawed if frozen

instructions: Rinse the peas and soak them until they soften, then drain. In a saucepan, combine the crushed stock cube, 200 ml of water, and bring to a simmer. Reduce heat and add the peas; cook for 10 minutes or until tender. While the peas are cooking, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, salt, pepper, and cumin in a bowl until no lumps remain.
When the peas are al dente, remove the pan from heat and stir in the rose water. Let the mixture cool a few minutes before transferring it to a spoon or a small ladle.

Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add a splash of olive oil. Drop spoonfuls of the pea mixture into the skillet, creating small dumplings. Cook for 3–4 minutes on each side until golden brown and crisp.
Serve the dumplings hot with a generous ladle of the warm vegetable broth, garnished with a drizzle of olive oil. This dish pairs wonderfully with a light salad or a splash of white wine.
If desired, steam the dumplings for an extra 5 minutes to achieve a softer texture.
